compiled_state.title: As a DSO practitioner, the Standard tier sonnet reviewer applies full 5-dimension checklists with researched sub-criteria

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Enhance the Standard tier sonnet code reviewer (code-reviewer-standard.md) with researched best-practice sub-criteria across all 5 scoring dimensions, incorporating project-specific patterns and reducing generic false positives.

**Why**: The Standard tier handles ~30-40% of reviews and performs comprehensive 5-dimension analysis. Its current checklist items are generic — they don't account for this project's conventions (bash hook scripts, Python ticket scripts, markdown skill files) or suppress findings already caught by pre-commit hooks (ruff, mypy).

**Scope**:
- IN: Updating all 5 dimension checklists in code-reviewer-standard.md with context-aware sub-criteria; adding file-type routing (bash vs Python vs markdown); researching current best practices for each dimension; adding project-pattern exclusions
- OUT: Changing scoring thresholds; modifying the output contract; adding new dimensions beyond the existing 5

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the Standard reviewer checklist contains file-type-aware sub-criteria that apply different checks for bash scripts (hooks/scripts), Python code (ticket scripts), and markdown files (skills/docs)
  ← Satisfies: 'Review Intelligence & Precision'
- When this story is complete, each dimension's checklist items include at least one project-specific pattern check (e.g., 'bash scripts must quote variables in conditionals', 'Python scripts must use subprocess instead of os.system')
  ← Satisfies: 'Review Intelligence & Precision'
- When this story is complete, unit tests verify the updated agent file structure and checklist completeness
  ← Satisfies: 'Review Intelligence & Precision'

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Delta file is composed with reviewer-base.md — changes must not duplicate base guidance
- [Performance] Standard tier uses sonnet — checklist length affects token usage and review time
- [Testing] Validate against sample diffs from recent commits to verify reduced false positive rate
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 2/6: Code patterns understood ✓ - Reviewed code-reviewer-standard.md (generated by build-review-agents.sh from reviewer-delta-standard.md), existing test patterns in tests/agents/ and tests/reviewers/, and assert.sh library.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 3/6: Tests written ✓ - Created tests/agents/test-reviewer-standard-checklist.sh with 12 assertions across: file-type routing (bash/Python/markdown), project-specific checks per dimension, no-duplicate-base-guidance. Currently failing 10/12 tests (RED state as expected).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 4/6: Implementation complete ✓ - Updated reviewer-delta-standard.md with: (1) File-Type Routing section for bash scripts, Python code, and markdown/skill files; (2) Project-specific sub-criteria per dimension (jq-free, fcntl.flock, .test-index, /dso: skill qualification, hook dispatcher architecture); (3) Checklist section dimension key mapping annotations. Regenerated code-reviewer-standard.md via build-review-agents.sh.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 5/6: Validation passed ✓ - Both test suites pass: test-reviewer-standard-checklist.sh (22/22) and test-reviewer-dimension-names.sh (6/6). Markdown structure verified: heading hierarchy OK, no unclosed code fences.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 6/6: Done ✓ - All acceptance criteria met: (1) File-type routing section covers bash/Python/markdown with appropriate sub-criteria per type; (2) Project-specific checks in all 5 dimensions: correctness (fcntl.flock, hook exit codes), verification (.test-index, RED markers), hygiene (jq-free), maintainability (skill qualification), design (dispatcher architecture); (3) Unit test file created at tests/agents/test-reviewer-standard-checklist.sh (22 assertions, all green). No base guidance duplicated. 5 canonical dimension keys preserved.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: Enhanced Standard tier reviewer with file-type routing, project-specific items across all 5 dimensions
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
